question: What is the default Linux operating system (OS) for AKS in Kubernetes versions 1.35 and above?
answer: The default Linux operating system (OS) for AKS in Kubernetes versions 1.35+ is Ubuntu 24.04.
supporting_sentences: AKS defaults to Ubuntu 24.04 in Kubernetes versions 1.35+.

question: Can you move an AKS cluster between Azure tenants?
answer: No, moving your AKS cluster between tenants is currently unsupported.
supporting_sentences: Moving your AKS cluster between tenants is currently unsupported.

question: Are Azure reservation discounts applicable to AKS agent nodes?
answer: Yes, Azure reservation discounts can be applied to AKS agent nodes because they are billed as standard Azure virtual machines (VMs).
supporting_sentences: AKS agent nodes are billed as standard Azure virtual machines (VMs).
For example, you can use most Azure VM types directly with AKS, and you can use Azure Reservations to receive discounts on those resources automatically.

question: What happens to the node resource group when an AKS cluster is deleted?
answer: The node resource group and all its resources are also deleted when the AKS cluster is deleted.
supporting_sentences: When you delete your cluster, the node resource group and all its resources are also deleted.

question: Which Windows Server version will be deprecated on AKS starting March 1, 2026?
answer: Starting on March 1, 2026, Windows Server 2019 node pools will no longer be supported on AKS.
supporting_sentences: Starting on March 01, 2026, Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) no longer supports Windows Server 2019 node pools.
